purpose: Operational registry of research sources for the superforecasting loop. Each market forecast must draw on > 5 DISPARATE sources (distinct organizations/source-types). Use this to (a) prefer primary/official sources, (b) weight by reliability, (c) balance ideological lean by pulling across the spectrum.

reliability_scale.5: Primary/official or non-partisan statistical authority; the data IS the resolution source or is the system of record.
reliability_scale.4: Wire service or nonpartisan aggregator with high factual-reporting record; minimal interpretation.
reliability_scale.3: Established outlet with reporting (verify claims vs a primary); apply bias balance.
reliability_scale.2: Opinion/analysis, partisan-commissioned, or market-signal (not fact); use as one input only, never alone.
reliability_scale.1: Low-reliability / unverified aggregator or AI-summary layer; must be cross-checked before use.

rating_basis: Reliability anchored to SOURCE TYPE plus external, methodology-based assessments: Ad Fontes Media (reliability score), AllSides (left/center/right bias), Media Bias/Fact Check (factual rating), and FiveThirtyEight/ABC pollster grades for pollsters. Ratings reflect those external frameworks, NOT the operator's political opinion. Bias labels are descriptive (where a source sits), not endorsements.
global_rules: > 5 disparate sources per market; record the list with the forecast.
Prefer reliability>=4 primary/official sources for any fact that drives the number.
Any single-source claim must be corroborated by a second, independent source before it enters a forecast (the EOWEEK 'June 11 EOs' artifact came from an uncorroborated AI search-summary).
For contested/political facts, pull at least one left-leaning AND one right-leaning outlet plus a center/wire source; do not let one lean dominate.
Betting/prediction markets are a signal (reliability 2-3), never a fact source; note them but weight by liquidity.
Treat the WebSearch result summary as reliability 1 (an AI compression layer) — open the underlying links / WebFetch the primary.

Sports human-decision markets (C1b): forecast the DECIDERS (voters, owners, league office), never the game; no single-game recency update (the no-single-poll rule); fan-sentiment-heavy markets are where longshot overpricing concentrates.

economics_data.name: BLS (Bureau of Labor Statistics) news releases
economics_data.use: CPI, U-3/employment situation; the resolution source for CPI/U-3 markets

economics_data.name: Cleveland Fed Inflation Nowcast
economics_data.use: forward MoM/YoY CPI & PCE nowcast (daily, oil+gas driven)

economics_data.name: EIA (Energy Information Administration)
economics_data.use: gasoline/energy prices feeding CPI energy component

economics_data.name: Trading Economics
economics_data.use: consensus forecasts + historical series (verify vs primary)

elections_politics.name: Official Secretary of State / election authority results
FiveThirtyEight pollster ratings + averages
elections_politics.use: weight individual polls by grade; discount partisan-commissioned polls

elections_politics.name: Local/state outlets (e.g. SD Searchlight, NonDoc, KOSU)
elections_politics.use: ground-level race dynamics, endorsements, vote redistribution

elections_politics.name: Polymarket / PredictIt (non-Kalshi)
elections_politics.use: independent market signal; weight by liquidity, never as fact

elections_politics.name: Cross-spectrum outlet pair (e.g. WSJ/Fox + NYT/WaPo + Al Jazeera)
elections_politics.bias: pull one left + one right + center
elections_politics.use: balance lean on contested political facts

film_tv_ratings.name: Rotten Tomatoes (Tomatometer page)
film_tv_ratings.use: THE resolution source for KXRT-* markets; read the live score + review count

film_tv_ratings.name: Box-office tracking (Box Office Mojo, The Numbers)

geopolitics_foreign.name: Primary statements (White House, State Dept, FIFA, foreign govts)
CFR / Brennan Center / nonpartisan think tanks
geopolitics_foreign.use: context, base rates, legal status (e.g. FISA certifications)

geopolitics_foreign.name: IMF PortWatch
geopolitics_foreign.use: resolution source for Strait-of-Hormuz transit-call markets

geopolitics_foreign.name: Cross-spectrum + regional outlets (Al Jazeera, BBC, WSJ)

sports_decisions.name: ESPN (news desk)
sports_decisions.use: award races, coach/personnel news, suspensions; fast but narrative-driven — corroborate insider scoops

sports_decisions.name: The Athletic
sports_decisions.use: deep beat reporting on personnel decisions; beat writers are the primary signal for front-office intent

sports_decisions.name: AP Sports
sports_decisions.use: confirmed transactions, rulings, retirements — treat as resolution-grade confirmation

sports_decisions.name: League official sites (NFL.com/NBA.com/MLB.com)
sports_decisions.use: official rulings, suspensions, transactions; THE resolution source for league decisions

sports_decisions.name: Spotrac / OverTheCap
sports_decisions.use: contract/cap mechanics that constrain personnel decisions (buyouts, guarantees, deadlines)

sports_decisions.name: Award ballot trackers (public voter polls)
sports_decisions.use: revealed-ballot tracking for MVP/HOF-style votes; small-n early, strong late — weight by ballots revealed

sports_decisions.name: Local beat reporters (team-market papers)
sports_decisions.use: earliest signal on coach/roster intent; single-source scoops need a second independent confirmation
